headline_summary: US–Israel strikes on Iran continued with expanded target sets and reports of intensified attacks in Tehran
Strait of Hormuz shipping disruption risk remained elevated, with tanker availability and war-risk insurance premia rising
Major carriers and shipowners adjusted routes/bookings amid security concerns, tightening global freight capacity
Cyber/information operations increased alongside kinetic operations, raising miscalculation and escalation risk
Markets remained focused on oil supply and shipping chokepoint risk translating into broader risk-off conditions
risk_drivers: Escalatory strike tempo and widening target sets
Persistent Strait of Hormuz disruption/insurance risk
Potential spillover to regional states and bases
Retaliation threats against international shipping and overseas assets

Energy: Upside skew for crude and refined products on Hormuz chokepoint and tanker insurance constraints (oil_up_risk)
Higher freight/war-risk premia for tankers and LNG; supply chain delays feed energy-price volatility
FX: Risk-off supports USD and safe-haven FX; pressure on EM/high-beta FX (em_fx_pressure, risk_off_flows)
Rates: Flight-to-quality can compress core yields while oil-shock inflation risk and uncertainty widen credit spreads (rates_credit_spreads)
Equities: Higher energy and freight costs weigh on cyclicals/transport; relative support for energy and defense

sources.title: Bombing of Tehran intensifies as war enters day six
US says Iran firing fewer missiles, US strikes to expand inland
Strait of Hormuz crisis: shipping, trade and oil markets

events.title: Vessel traffic through Strait of Hormuz nearly halted; tankers reported stranded
events.implication: Throughput shock risk for crude/LNG; war-risk premia and freight/insurance surge (oil_up_risk).

events.title: Blast damages Bahamas-flagged tanker Sonangol Namibe near Khor Al Zubair port
events.implication: Signals spillover risk into Iraqi export infrastructure and shipping lanes; supports oil_up_risk.

events.title: Turkey says NATO air defences destroyed a ballistic missile fired from Iran; debris reported in Hatay
events.implication: Potential widening involving a NATO member increases escalation tail-risk and supports risk_off_flows.

events.title: COSCO Shipping suspends new bookings for Middle East routes amid conflict
events.implication: Carrier booking suspensions tighten capacity and can compound trade delays and freight inflation.

events.title: Tanker transit reported to load Murban crude via Strait of Hormuz amid disruption (Pola to Jebel Dhanna)
events.implication: Partial flows continue but under elevated risk; reinforces optionality value for alternative routes and stocks.

events.title: War-risk insurance premia spike for Gulf shipping amid Hormuz threats
events.implication: Higher insurance costs and reduced vessel availability amplify oil_up_risk and global freight inflation.

events.title: US announces additional sanctions targeting Iranian-linked entities amid conflict escalation
events.implication: Sanctions tighten financing/shipping channels and can further constrain Iranian crude flows and regional trade.

events.title: Iranian state TV feed disrupted; opposition figure aired in apparent hack
events.implication: Information operations raise instability/miscalculation risk and can accompany kinetic escalation.

events.title: US says Iran firing fewer missiles; US strikes to expand inland
events.implication: Signals intent to widen target sets; keeps escalation risk elevated and supports risk_off_flows.

events.title: Bombing of Tehran intensifies as war enters day six
events.implication: Sustained air campaign elevates retaliation and spillover risk, reinforcing oil_up_risk and risk_off_flows.

events.title: Major shipping companies suspend operations through the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Capacity withdrawal drives freight spikes and raises energy delivery uncertainty; supports oil_up_risk.

events.title: Iran says it will attack ships attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Explicit threat heightens chokepoint disruption pricing and boosts war-risk premia (oil_up_risk).

events.title: Reports of multiple ships hit and traffic collapse in Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Damage and reduced traffic can rapidly tighten physical crude supply and lift freight (oil_up_risk).

events.title: US lawmakers debate measures to constrain or halt US air campaign against Iran
events.implication: Domestic political constraints can shape conflict duration and perceived escalation risk; market impact via risk premium.

events.title: GCaptain analysis: tanker markets brace for historic shock from Hormuz disruption
events.implication: Market structure stress (vessel scarcity, dislocation) can magnify crude price moves beyond immediate supply loss.

links.from: Vessel traffic through Strait of Hormuz nearly halted; tankers reported stranded
links.why: A chokepoint throughput shock threatens crude and LNG exports, lifting supply risk premia.

links.from: War-risk insurance premia spike for Gulf shipping amid Hormuz threats
links.why: Higher insurance costs and reduced vessel willingness constrain effective shipping capacity.

links.from: Major shipping companies suspend operations through the Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Carrier suspension increases probability of delayed/curtailed hydrocarbon flows.

links.from: Bombing of Tehran intensifies as war enters day six
links.why: Sustained strikes increase tail-risk of regional escalation, prompting de-risking.

links.from: US says Iran firing fewer missiles; US strikes to expand inland
links.why: Signals widening target set and conflict duration uncertainty.

links.from: Turkey says NATO air defences destroyed a ballistic missile fired from Iran; debris reported in Hatay
links.why: Involving a NATO member raises escalation risk beyond core belligerents.
Risk aversion typically weakens higher-beta EM FX and supports safe havens.
Oil shocks raise inflation uncertainty and can widen corporate/sovereign spreads via growth hit.

links.from: Blast damages Bahamas-flagged tanker Sonangol Namibe near Khor Al Zubair port
links.to: Vessel traffic through Strait of Hormuz nearly halted; tankers reported stranded
links.why: Attacks near export ports add to perception of broad Gulf shipping danger and capacity pullback.

links.from: Iran says it will attack ships attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz
links.to: War-risk insurance premia spike for Gulf shipping amid Hormuz threats
links.why: Explicit threats directly increase insurer and shipowner risk pricing.
